intended_use: CT VScore+ is a software application intended for non-invasive evaluation of calcified lesions of the coronary arteries based on ECG-gated, non-contrast cardiac CT images for patients aged 30 years or
intended_use_full: CT VScore+ is a software application intended for non-invasive evaluation of calcified lesions of the coronary arteries based on ECG-gated, non-contrast cardiac CT images for patients aged 30 years or older. The device automatically generates calcium scores for the coronary arteries (combined LM+LAD, RCA, LCX) and highlights the segmented calcium on the original CT image.
target_condition: Coronary artery calcification; calcified lesions of coronary arteries
ground_truth: Consensus manual scoring on FDA-cleared device (Vitrea CT VScore, K243240) and 2+1 consensus process by three U.S. board-certified radiologists/cardiologists
other_metrics: Total Agatston Score ICC(2,1): 0.997 [95% CI: 0.996–0.998]; Total Volume Score ICC(2,1): 0.996 [95% CI: 0.995–0.997]; Per-Vessel ICCs: LCx 0.937, RCA 0.990, LM+LAD 0.983; CAC-DRS 4-Class Kappa: 0.959 [95% CI: 0.936–0.982]; CAC Standard 5-Class Kappa: 0.958 [95% CI: 0.938–0.978]; Voxelwise Dice Score: 0.920 overall (per-vessel: LCx 0.874, RCA 0.883, LM+LAD 0.958)
